LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT MURPHY'S DELI FRANCHISING INC.'S MOTION PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 425.16 TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF CROSS-COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. CROSS-COMPLAINT ARISES OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY AND IS DESIGNED TO INHIBIT LITIGATION. AN ACT IN FURTHERANCE OF A FIRST AMENDMENT RIGHT COMPRISES BOTH A RIGHT TO SPEAK FREELY AND ALSO A RIGHT TO REFRAIN FROM DOING SO AT ALL. ARP PHARMACY SERVICES, INC. VS. GALLAGHER BASSETT SERVICES, INC. (2006) 138 CAL.APP.4TH 1307, 1314. HERE THE GRAVAMEN OF NOP'S CROSS-DEFENDANTS IS CROSS-DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO PREVENT THE FILING OF THE UNDERLYING COMPLAINT. CROSS COMPLAINANT FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE THE PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON ITS CLAIMS. CROSS-COMPLAINANT MUST DEMONSTRATE THAT THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS BOTH LEGALLY SUFFICIENT AND SUPPORTED BY A PRIMA FACIE SHOWING OF FACTS TO SUSTAIN A FAVORABLE JUDGMENT. FELDMAN VS. 1100 PARK LANE ASSOCIATES (2008) 160 CAL.APP.4TH 1467. THERE IS NO ALLEGATION OF A PARTICULAR BREACH OF THE LEASE BY MURPHY'S THAT WOULD GIVE RISE TO A BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION OR FACTS PLED TO TRIGGER AN INDEMNITY PROVISION. NO FACTS ALLEGED DESCRIBING A CONTROVERSY THAT WOULD SUPPORT A DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS BARRED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE IN THAT IT IS BASED ON ALLEGEDLY WRONGFUL CONDUCT BY CROSS-DEFENDANTS' FAILURE TO PREVENT A LAWSUIT AND ITS PURPOSE WAS DESIGNED TO CHILL FREE ACCESS TO THE COURTS. CROSS-COMPLAINT'S NOTICE OF ERRATA NOT CONSIDERED AS UNTIMELY. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENANTS GEORGE AND JEANETTE OMRAN'S MOTION PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 425.16 TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF CROSS-COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. CROSS-COMPLAINT ARISES OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY AND IS DESIGNED TO INHIBIT LITIGATION. CROSS-COMPLAINANT FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE THE PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON ITS CLAIMS. CROSS-COMPLAINANT MUST DEMONSTRATE THAT THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS BOTH LEGALLY SUFFICIENT AND SUPPORTED BY A PRIMA FACIE SHOWING OF FACTS TO SUSTAIN A FAVORABLE JUDGMENT. FELDMAN VS. 1100 PARK LANE ASSOCIATES (2008) 160 CAL.APP.4TH 1467. THERE IS NO ALLEGATION OF A PARTICULAR BREACH OF THE LEASE BY CAL-MURPHY THAT WOULD TRIGGER THE GUARANTY AGREEMENT AND ESTABLISH A BREACH. NO FACTS ALLEGED GIVING RISE TO A CONTROVERSY THAT WOULD SUPPORT A DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS BARRED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE IN THAT IT IS BASED ON ALLEGEDLY WRONGFUL CONDUCT BY CROSS-DEFENDANTS' FAILURE TO PREVENT A LAWSUIT, AND ITS PURPOSE WAS DESIGNED TO CHILL FREE ACCESS TO THE COURTS. CROSS-COMPLAINT'S NOTICE OF ERRATA NOT CONSIDERED AS UNTIMELY. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

The court awards $3,045.75 in costs. Total attorneys fees and costs is $45,545.75. Order signed in open Court. MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT NOP 560 MISSION LLC, and, HINES INTERESTS L. P. DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Diana L. Attorney at Law, (415) 362-1365, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party. Hearing held, (no argument on substantive issues). The Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained with thirty days leave to amend. As to the Third Cause of Action for breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing, plaintiff must plead the written contract word for word, or generally according to its legal intentment and effect, or attach a copy and incorporate it by reference. The Eighth Cause of Action for recession must specify the relationships of the parties, the existence of a contract, and the contractual obligation that would permit a recession claim. Motion Pursuant To Ccp Section 425.16 To Strike Portions Of Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-11-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENANTS GEORGE AND JEANETTE OMRAN'S Motion Pursuant To CCP Section 425.16 to Strike Portions of Cross-Complaint. Appearances: Gail E. Kavanagh, Esq. of Sedwick Detert, (415) 781-7900, appearing for Cross complainant NOP 560 Mission LLC, and opposing party; Herbert W. Yanowitz, Esq. Attorney at Law, appearing for Cross-defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Granted. Cross-complaint arises out of protected activity and is designed to inhibit litigation. An act in furtherance of a first amendment right comprises both a right to speak freely and also a right to refrain from doing so at all. ARP Pharmacy Services, Inc. vs. Gallagher Bassett Services, Inc. (2006) 138 Cal. App. 4th 1307, 1314. Here the gravamen of NOP's cross-complaint is defendants' failure to prevent the filing of the underlying complaint. Cross-complainant fails to demonstrate the probability of prevailing on its claims. Cross-complainant must demonstrate that the cross-complaint is both legally sufficient and supported by a prima facie showing of facts to sustain a favorable judgment. Feldman vs. 1100 Park Lane Associates (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 1467. There is no allegation of a particular breach of the lease by Cal-Murphy that would trigger the Guaranty Agreement and establish a breach. No facts alleged giving rise to a controversy that would support a declaratory relief cause of action. The cross-complaint is barred by the litigation privilege in that it is based on allegedly wrongful conduct by cross-defendants' failure to prevent a lawsuit, and its purpose was designed to chill free access to the courts. Cross-complaint's Notice of Errata not considered as untimely. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

Cross-complaint arises out of protected activity and is designed to inhibit litigation. An act in furtherance of a first amendment right comprises both a right to speak freely and also a right to refrain from doing so at all. ARP Pharmacy Services, Inc. vs. Gallagher Bassett Services, Inc. (2006) 138 Cal.App.4th 1307, 1314. Here the gravamen of NOP's cross-defendants is cross-defendant's failure to prevent the filing of the underlying complaint. Cross complainant fails to demonstrate the probability of prevailing on its claims. Cross-complainant must demonstrate that the cross-complaint is both legally sufficient and supported by a prima facie showing of facts to sustain a favorable judgment.
